title: World
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Democrats to force vote in Congress to limit Trump’s war powers against Iran – US politics live
items.description: Lawmakers are seeking the US president to require congressional approval before further military action against Iran Sign up for the Breaking News US ...

items.title: EU considers response to Middle East conflict as countries prepare to evacuate citizens – Europe live
items.description: EU’s top diplomat, Kaja Kallas, warns against further escalation following US and Israel strikes on Iran A Cypriot government spokesperson has just co...

items.title: UK house prices rise in February as chancellor avoids ‘negative speculation’
items.description: Rachel Reeves’s upcoming spring forecast has not led to slowdown, as property tax rumours did in November Business live – latest updates UK politics l...

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Kuwait mistakenly shoots down three US fighter planes, as US says Iran’s ‘reckless’ attacks threaten regional stability
items.description: Israeli military says fighting Iran-backed Hezbollah could take ‘many’ more days; US crew bailed out safely after crash-landing, says Kuwait Podcast: ...

items.title: RAF airbase in Cyprus hit, two more drones shot down as Cooper defends decision to allow US to use UK bases for Iran strikes – UK politics live
items.description: Foreign secretary confirms strike on Akrotiri base but says it ‘simply not true’ the UK is being dragged into an Iraq-style conflict ‘Simply not true’...

items.title: Two drones headed to RAF base intercepted, Cyprus says
items.description: No casualties were reported and the base sustained "minimal damage", the Ministry of Defence said.

items.title: UK will allow US to use bases to strike Iranian missile sites, PM says
items.description: The prime minister says it remains the case that the UK will "not join offensive action now".

items.title: Reshuffled flights and players stuck - how sport is navigating Middle East crisis
items.description: BBC Sport looks at how the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is affecting the staging of sporting events around the world.

items.title: Two people hurt in Edinburgh as armed police called to reports of man with knife
items.description: The two people have been taken to the Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh and police say there is not believed to be any wider risk to the public.

items.title: Refugee status becomes temporary in asylum shake-up
items.description: Adults and accompanied children claiming asylum will only receive refugee status for 30 months under the changes.
